currentPlan.title: GTA Like Game
currentPlan.summary: SCALE & GRID STRATEGY: Using the humanoid scale logic where 1 Character Unit (H) = 5 studs, we calculate sizes for objects and roads. For example, a car is 3H long (15 studs), a road lane is 2.4H wide (12 studs), and a story is 2.4H tall (12 studs). The ground level is determined by Ground_Y = Baseplate.Position.Y + (Baseplate.Size.Y / 2), and object positions are snapped using the formula Object.Position.Y = Ground_Y + (Object.Size.Y / 2).

steps.description: Create the ground/baseplate: Size [512, 1, 512] at [0, -0.5, 0]. Material: Grass.
Insert a main highway road using insert_free_model with assetId 106470926724188. Size to [12, 1, 200] at [0, 0.5, 0].
Insert a skyscraper building using insert_free_model with assetId 106470926724188. Position at [60, Ground_Y + 12/2, 0] using the snap formula.
Insert a police car vehicle using insert_free_model with assetId 6418230807. Position at [0, Ground_Y + 7.5, 30] using the snap formula.
Insert a Mercedes vehicle using insert_free_model with assetId 6527733561. Position at [15, Ground_Y + 7.5, 30] using the snap formula.
Create a Script in ServerScriptService for player spawning and game logic.
Create a LocalScript in StarterScripts for client-side input handling and UI updates.
Design and implement a UI system for displaying player stats, mini-map, and other game information.
Implement a traffic system with AI-controlled vehicles using the police car and Mercedes models.
Add a day-night cycle and weather system to enhance gameplay and immersion.
Create a mission system with objectives and rewards for players to complete.
Implement a save system to store player progress and game state.
